title: Target
excerpt: A target is the creature, object, space, point, area, or other valid focus that an attack, spell, feature, hazard, or interaction is resolving against.
markdown:
# Target

A target is the creature, object, space, point, area, or other valid focus that an effect is resolving against. A target is not limited to spells. Attacks, features, hazards, interactions, and environmental effects can all resolve against targets.

## Rules Use

### Valid Focus Of Resolution
A rule determines what counts as a valid target for that specific effect. Some effects require a creature, some require an object, some choose a point in space, and some affect an area without selecting a single creature first.

### Targeting Is Narrower Than Reach
Being within range, reach, line of effect, or line of sight does not automatically make something a valid target. The effect still has to permit that kind of target.

### Effects Can Use Different Target Shapes
An effect can target:
- A single creature
- Multiple creatures
- An object
- A point in space
- A defined [[area_of_effect|area of effect]]
- The source itself

### Chosen Target Versus Affected Target
Some effects choose a target directly. Others choose a point, space, or area and then affect every valid creature or object inside it. In those cases, the chosen focus and the affected targets are not always the same thing.

## Resolution Notes

- A target must satisfy the effect's own conditions at the time of resolution
- If a target becomes invalid before resolution, the effect can fail, redirect, or resolve only against the remaining valid targets depending on the governing rule
- Filters such as creature type, attitude, visibility, cover, or current state can further narrow what counts as a valid target

## Common Uses

- Making an attack against a creature or object
- Choosing a point where a spell or hazard begins
- Applying a feature to an ally, enemy, or controlled creature
- Resolving an interaction against a door, lever, item, or other world object
